title: Oxylobium racemosum
taxonomy: Oxylobium racemosum is classified as a flowering plant, specifically a member of the family Fabaceae. More specifically, it belongs to the subfamily Faboideae. It is also known to be a part of the genus Oxylobium, which is a group of flowering plants native to Australia. Oxylobium racemosum is further classified as a species within this genus. Its classification is often used in botanical studies and research on the plant's characteristics and distribution.

intro: Oxylobium racemosum, commonly known as the native pea or native broom, is a perennial flowering plant native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant is used in traditional medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various skin conditions and infections. The bioactive constituents of Oxylobium racemosum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including eczema, dermatitis, and wounds.
